title: moquinia polymorpha var. ceanothifolia
intro: Moquinia polymorpha var. ceanothifolia, also known as West Indian jasmine or mountain rose, is a flowering plant native to the Caribbean and Central America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-anxiety effects. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Moquinia polymorpha var. ceanothifolia are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
